manufacturer_evidence: An instant classic, the Big Baron is inspired by the mid lengths that generations before us rode on the infamous Gold Coast points. This is paired with a more modern bottom contour and twin fin placement, then complemented by a neatly pulled in round tail for extra hold. Because on this board, you’ll be burning speed and burying rail. A lot of it.
rocker_notes: Because on this board, you’ll be burning speed and burying rail.

manufacturer_evidence: Our Big Baron has been a global favourite for surfers of all ability levels for several years, and now we’ve scaled into even more generous EasyRider Dimensions. Along with a wider outline in the nose and tail, this creates an incredibly stable, forgiving board. Featuring a deck handle on all sizes 6’8”and above plus a centre line on the deck to make positioning simple for anyone, every single detail on the Big Baron EasyRider softboard is geared towards making each stage of surfing easier than ever. Compared to all of our other softboard models, the Big Baron EasyRider also has the flattest deck in the range to maintain volume and buoyancy. To build the best soft boards possible, we’ve kept it simple. Proven JS small wave shapes that you can actually manoeuvre, built to maximize durability and safety. It starts with the same top-quality materials and construction processes we use in our world-renowned JS range, including a Carbon T-stringer, 6oz+4oz glass on the deck and 6oz glass on the bottom. It is finished with a soft 3mm PE shell on the deck, plus a reinforced HD PE bottom.
tail_notes: Along with a wider outline in the nose and tail, this creates an incredibly stable, forgiving board. Featuring a deck handle on all sizes 6’8”and above plus a centre line on the deck to make positioning simple for anyone, every single detail on the Big Baron EasyRider softboard is geared towards making each stage of surfing easier than ever.

manufacturer_evidence: The head honcho of the Baron family – the El Barón features a stretched out Big Baron outline that’s ridden narrower, with slightly more rocker and modern concaves to handle more speed and versatility. It’s a subtle single to double, featuring a “reverse concave” – one of Jason’s favourite designs. This means it’s a deeper concave in the middle, with slightly shallower concave in the nose and tail to help with water flow for maximum drive. Similar to the Big Baron, riding El Barón will naturally smooth out your surfing and translate almost immediately to the shorter, more high-performance boards that most of us want to ride when conditions (and mood) align.
rocker_notes: The head honcho of the Baron family – the El Barón features a stretched out Big Baron outline that’s ridden narrower, with slightly more rocker and modern concaves to handle more speed and versatility.
tail_notes: This means it’s a deeper concave in the middle, with slightly shallower concave in the nose and tail to help with water flow for maximum drive.

manufacturer_evidence: The Forget Me Not 3 has been fine tuned for the waves we all dream about. Whether it’s head high with perfect shape, or solid tubes, it’s our most refined model for unlimited performance in all hollow conditions. For over a decade now, the DNA of this board has excelled on countless surf trips and each of the best quality locations on the Championship Tour. From Parko and Julian becoming Pipe Masters and Triple Crown champions, to Dusty winning at Haleiwa, Owen dominating at Cloudbreak (including two perfect 20 point heats along the way), and Jeremy, Ace and Julian all taking top honours at Teahupo’o.
